display.purpose: Page-level h1s: notebook atlas title, source titles.

headline.purpose: Section h2s: region headers, dialog titles.

body.purpose: Source Markdown content and long-form prose (page intros, dialog copy, provider notes).

label.purpose: Coordinate labels, source order/word-count, timestamps, legends — data annotations, not prose.

shadows.name: stamp
shadows.purpose: Notebook cards — hard offset, no blur, reads as an ink stamp on paper.

shadows.name: stamp-quiet
shadows.purpose: Provider settings cards — the same stamp move at lower opacity.

shadows.name: sheet-lift
shadows.purpose: The dialog card only — the one element genuinely floating above the page.

motion.name: survey-pulse
motion.purpose: Loading indicator: a breathing dot (scale 1 to 0.65, opacity 1 to 0.25) shown ahead of loading copy.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The default call-to-action across the app (create notebook, submit forms).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--blueprint); border-radius: 3px; background: var(--blueprint); color: var(--surface); padding: 0.7rem 1rem; font-weight: 680; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #193f98; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--vermilion); outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { cursor: wait; opacity: 0.58; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Low-emphasis actions: cancel, retry, inline row actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--ink); border-radius: 3px; background: var(--surface); color: var(--ink); padding: 0.7rem 1rem; font-weight: 680; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--paper); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--vermilion); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive actions only: delete notebook, delete source.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--vermilion); border-radius: 3px; background: var(--vermilion); color: var(--surface); padding: 0.7rem 1rem; font-weight: 680; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: #a8301f; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--vermilion); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Ruled-line field with an uppercase monospace label above it, styled as a field-tag rather than a soft placeholder treatment.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 0.35rem; } .ds-label { font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 720; letter-spacing: 0.055em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--ink); } .ds-input { width: 100%; border: 1px solid var(--line); border-bottom-color: var(--ink); border-radius: 2px; background: var(--surface); color: var(--ink); padding: 0.75rem 0.8rem; font: inherit; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--vermilion); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Notebook Card
components.description: The atlas grid's signature card: a stamped shadow, a low-opacity plate number, and a title styled like a map entry.
components.html:
01The Ember Coast

Updated Jul 12
components.css: .ds-notebook-card { position: relative; min-height: 175px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid var(--ink); background: var(--surface); padding: 1.25rem; box-shadow: 5px 5px 0 rgb(36 87 197 / 15%); font-family: 'Archivo Variable', Archivo, sans-serif; } .ds-map-index { position: absolute; top: 0.45rem; right: 0.65rem; color: rgb(36 87 197 / 16%); font-size: 4.5rem; font-weight: 800; line-height: 1; } .ds-notebook-link { position: relative; display: block; max-width: 85%; margin: 1.4rem 0 0.75rem; color: var(--ink); font-size: 1.35rem; font-weight: 740; line-height: 1.1; text-decoration-color: var(--vermilion); } .ds-notebook-updated { margin: 0; color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.8rem; font-family: 'Source Serif 4 Variable', Georgia, serif; }

components.name: Confirm Dialog
components.description: Modal confirmation sheet: monospace eyebrow, serif copy, right-aligned actions. The one component that earns a soft ambient shadow.
components.html:
Confirm action

Delete notebook?

Delete The Ember Coast and every Markdown source inside it? This cannot be undone.
components.css: .ds-dialog-card { width: min(100%, 620px); border: 1px solid var(--ink); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--surface); padding: clamp(1.1rem, 5vw, 2rem); box-shadow: 0 18px 50px rgb(23 33 43 / 14%); font-family: 'Archivo Variable', Archivo, sans-serif; } .ds-coordinate-label { margin: 0 0 0.35rem; color: var(--muted); font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 650; letter-spacing: 0.095em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-dialog-title { margin: 0 0 0.65rem; font-weight: 700; } .ds-dialog-copy { font-family: 'Source Serif 4 Variable', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.05rem; color: var(--ink); } .ds-dialog-actions { display: flex; justify-content: flex-end; gap: 0.65rem; margin-top: 1.2rem; } .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--ink); border-radius: 3px; background: var(--surface); color: var(--ink); padding: 0.7rem 1rem; font-weight: 680; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-danger { min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--vermilion); border-radius: 3px; background: var(--vermilion); color: var(--surface); padding: 0.7rem 1rem; font-weight: 680; cursor: pointer; }

components.name: Source List Item
components.description: The signature spine component: a colored bar acting as a book-spine/survey-flag state indicator, lichen by default and vermilion when active — not a decorative border accent.
components.html: • 01Coastal Trade Routes2 days ago
components.css: .ds-source-list { margin: 0; padding: 0; list-style: none; font-family: 'Archivo Variable', Archivo, sans-serif; } .ds-source-item { position: relative; display: grid; grid-template-columns: 2rem 1fr auto; gap: 0.3rem 0.6rem; min-height: 72px; align-items: center; color: var(--ink); padding: 0.7rem 0.45rem 0.7rem 0.8rem; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); } .ds-source-item:hover, .ds-source-item.ds-source-item-active { background: rgb(36 87 197 / 8%); } .ds-source-spine { position: absolute; inset: 0 auto 0 0; width: 4px; background: var(--lichen); } .ds-source-item-active .ds-source-spine { background: var(--vermilion); } .ds-source-order { grid-row: 1 / 3; font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 650; letter-spacing: 0.095em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); } .ds-source-title { overflow: hidden; font-weight: 690; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-source-updated { grid-column: 3; color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.68rem; white-space: nowrap; }

narrative.overview:
worldbookllm reads like a working reference document, not a screen designed to be admired: cool paper stock under a faint blueprint grid, coordinate-style labels, indexed entries, and stamped-flat cards that behave like survey marks rather than floating tiles. It is built for use in the field — legible under scrutiny, precise about what it shows — not for display on a shelf. The metaphor is deliberate: a notebook is unmapped territory being surveyed and logged (the "atlas" of notebooks, "charting" as a loading state, "plotted territories" as an empty state, "route interrupted" as an error state), and every screen keeps that cartographic voice consistent rather than reaching for generic app chrome.

This system explicitly rejects the generic-SaaS-dashboard look — no cream/sand card grids, no gradient text, no hero-metric tiles — and rejects the stock-AI-chatbot skin, where a bare message-bubble list dominates the screen. Sources and their Markdown stay visually primary; chat is one region among several, reached for rather than defaulted into.
keyCharacteristics: Cool, slightly green-grey paper background with a faint blueprint-blue grid, never a warm cream/sand neutral
Serif body copy (Source Serif 4) against sans-serif structural type (Archivo) — an editorial contrast pairing, not two similar sans faces
Flat, hard-edged cards with offset "stamp" shadows instead of soft blur — the system's signature elevation move
Monospace, uppercase, letter-spaced micro-labels for anything that behaves like metadata (coordinates, counts, timestamps, indices)
Two accent colors doing distinct jobs: blueprint for navigation/primary action, vermilion for danger/focus/active state — never interchangeable

rules.name: The Two-Accent Rule
rules.body: Blueprint and vermilion never swap jobs. Blueprint means "go / select / structural"; vermilion means "danger / focus / active-right-now." A screen that needs a third signal reaches for lichen, not a new hue.

rules.name: The Serif-Is-Canon Rule
rules.body: Source Serif 4 is reserved for content that came from — or reads like — the user's own material: Markdown bodies, page intros, dialog copy, provider notes. Archivo owns UI chrome, controls, and structural headings. If a string is describing the app, it's Archivo; if it's the user's words or long-form reading, it's the serif.

rules.name: The One Soft Shadow Rule
rules.body: Blur is reserved for the dialog sheet, the only element genuinely floating above the page. Every other elevated surface uses a hard offset instead — if it isn't overlaying the page, it doesn't get blur.

dos: Do keep the page background a cool, desaturated paper (#e8e9e3) with the faint blueprint grid — never a warm cream or sand neutral.
Do pair Archivo (UI/structure) against Source Serif 4 (user content/long-form prose) — keep that split consistent; don't let Archivo creep into Markdown bodies or vice versa.
Do use hard offset "stamp" shadows (no blur) on any card or button that sits flat on the page; reserve soft blurred shadows for the dialog sheet alone.
Do use monospace, uppercase, letter-spaced labels for anything that is metadata (counts, dates, coordinates, indices) — this is the system's substitute for iconography.
Do keep blueprint for primary/structural and vermilion for danger/focus/active — the Two-Accent Rule.
Do keep sources and Markdown content visually primary; chat regions are reached for, never the default full-bleed layout.
donts: Don't use a cream/sand/beige body background, gradient text, glassmorphism, or a hero-metric-tile layout — this should never read as a generic SaaS dashboard.
Don't default to a bare chat-bubble layout where the message list dominates the screen — that's the stock-AI-chatbot skin this system explicitly avoids.
Don't add soft blurred shadows to cards, buttons, or list items — blur is reserved for the one element actually floating above the page (the dialog sheet).

Don't use border-left/border-right as a decorative colored accent on cards or callouts. The source-list spine is the one sanctioned exception, and it functions as a state indicator (default vs. active), not decoration.
Don't round corners on notebook/provider cards or buttons beyond 3-4px — square-cornered precision is the point, not softness.

Don't let placeholder or muted text drop below the 4.5:1 contrast minimum against paper or surface; the muted blue-grey (#5a656d) is already tuned to that floor — don't lighten it further "for elegance."
